title: Create mirroring bash script
instructions: Create a Bash script at SYSTEM/mirdir.sh that mirrors two directories specified by the command‑line options -s <source> and -d <destination>. The script should validate inputs, display help, and perform a true mirror (including deletions) using rsync.

1779837879.1024337.instruction: Create SYSTEM/mirdir.sh with a shebang line and a placeholder for usage help. Ensure the file has executable permissions.

1779837879.1053526.instruction: Implement option parsing to accept -s <source> and -d <destination>. Include a -h or --help flag that prints usage information. Validate that both -s and -d are provided and that the source directory exists.

1779837879.1278207.instruction: Check whether the destination directory exists. If not, create it. If it exists, ensure it is a directory. Handle errors gracefully.

log.text: Created SYSTEM/mirdir.sh with shebang and basic structure including usage help.

1779837879.130856.instruction: Use rsync with the following options: -a (archive), --delete (remove files in dest not in src), -v (verbose). Construct the command as `rsync -av --delete "$SRC"/ "$DST"/` ensuring trailing slashes for correct behavior.

log.text: Implemented rsync command with -a, --delete, and -v options. Ensured trailing slashes for correct behavior.

1779837879.13324.instruction: After rsync completes, exit with the same status code. If any validation fails, exit with a non‑zero status and an appropriate error message.

log.text: Added exit status handling to propagate rsync exit code. Implemented error handling for validation failures with appropriate error messages and non-zero exit codes.

1779837879.1357334.instruction: Add descriptive comments throughout the script explaining each section and the purpose of key variables and commands.

log.text: Added comprehensive comments to explain the script structure, variable purposes, and command functions.
